meta.note: Listening comprehension sets (spec §3.3): short Taiwan-scene dialogues with questions. Kept within curriculum + HSK 1 vocabulary. Audio is synthesised at request time (edge-tts), alternating the two zh-TW voices.

dialogue.speaker: 客人
dialogue.hanzi: 不好意思，請問有沒有便當？
dialogue.gloss: Excuse me, do you have boxed meals?

dialogue.speaker: 店員
dialogue.gloss: Yes, the boxed meals are over there.

dialogue.speaker: 客人
dialogue.hanzi: 我要一個便當和一瓶水。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ yào yí ge biàndāng hàn yì píng shuǐ.
dialogue.gloss: I'll have one boxed meal and a bottle of water.

options.text: A boxed meal and a bottle of water

dialogue.speaker: 店員
dialogue.gloss: Welcome, what would you like to drink?

dialogue.speaker: 客人
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ yào yì bēi zhēnzhū nǎichá.
dialogue.gloss: I'd like a bubble milk tea.

dialogue.speaker: 客人
dialogue.gloss: Half sugar, no ice. To go.

questions.prompt: What sweetness and ice do they want?

dialogue.speaker: 我
dialogue.pinyin: Duì, bù lěng yě bú rè.
dialogue.gloss: Right, not cold and not hot.

dialogue.speaker: 朋友
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒmen qù hē chá, hǎo ma?
dialogue.gloss: Let's go have some tea, okay?

dialogue.speaker: 我
dialogue.pinyin: Hǎo, wǒ hěn xǐhuān hē chá.
dialogue.gloss: Sure, I really like drinking tea.

options.text: Nice — not cold and not hot

questions.prompt: What do they decide to do?
